publishedAbstract: By default, ODF deploys Ceph RGW with support only for S3 path-style bucket access. AWS originally planned to deprecate path-style access in 2020, replacing it with virtual host-style access. See [AWS virtual host documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/VirtualHosting.html) for more info. As a result, many S3 libraries and applications default to virtual host-style access. It is a user burden to change configurations for their OpenShift-based S3 client applications. Some applications no longer support path-style access at all. As an added complication, AWS S3 provides ubiquitous support for TLS (HTTPS). In the same way that modern S3 clients assume virtual host-style access, TLS-secured access is often an equivalent assumption. This solution guide will allow user S3 clients to access ODF RGW S3 storage using virtual host-style access. Further, the solution here is configured with a focus on TLS. S3 client Pods will have access to the required TLS certificates without any special modifications to application Pods.
publishedTitle: OpenShift Data Foundation - Enable internal virtual host-style URLs for S3 RGW buckets (Developer Preview)
